calendar.matters:
Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement Probate Code 17200(B)
Off Calendar Jul-03-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Schultz appeared by zoom for and with Scott Hodges (trustee) by zoom.
Steven Kahn (litigation counsel) appeared by zoom for and with Scott Hodges (trustee) by zoom.
Shaun Carberry appeared in person and Aleksandr Volkov appeared by zoom for Diana Tong (beneficiary).
Vladie Viltman appeared in person for the Hinns (plaintiffs in the underlying litigation).
William Keeler appeared by zoom for Cathy Yih.

The court noted that a civil proceeding is scheduled in Dept. 501, and asked if the parties have met and conferred regarding anyone having a road map for resolution. Discussion was held regarding documents having been produced by Mr. Hodges and his deposition having been taken, Mr. Volkon's position that issues still exist because Mr. Hodges was told not to answer questions regarding the insurance policy, and his filings being rejected because he does not show as attorney of record, whether it is appropriate for the court to approve the settlement agreement at this time, whether or not the court should continue the matter to allow Mr. Carberry and Mr. Volkon time to conduct further discovery, discovery being closed, the issue of Mr. Hodges' insurance relating to Mrs. Tong's potential claim of breach of fiduciary duty and not relating to the petition for approval of the settlement agreement, Mr. Hodges being sued as a trustee and not as an individual, whether approval of the settlement will impact Mrs. Tong's ability to bring future claims, Mrs. Tong not having been part of the settlement agreement, etc. The Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Reporter: Judy de Alba, CSR # 5709, 650-571-1473, judy@dealbareportingservice.com

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-04-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Conrad for and with (trustee) Scott Hodges. Andrew Schultz for and with Scott Hodges. Steven Heller for and with (objector) Diana Tom.

The Court stated that this is the trustee's third account and report, that there have been objections and replies and asked if counsel had anything to add. Mr. Heller stated that he senses the court has a handle on this. The Court stated that it understands from the reply that this accounting does not seek fees for the property manager. Mr. Schultz confirmed this and added that it will be in the next accounting. Mr. Conrad addressed his fees stating that all of the work helped produce income for the trust, that Mr. Hodges had to file a partition action, that Ms. Tom was dilatory in getting the documents to them for the loan, that they obtained the loan and were able to make repairs to the property to get more rent, that he helped Mr. Hodges negotiate a new leas on the store and collected back rent on the Deli. Mr. Heller stated that they dispute the characterization that Ms. Tom was dilatory and held up the loan, that they feel it was Mr. Hodges fault, that there are two sets of attorneys being paid fees of over $35,000.00 for one year, that they were previously paid a large amount and that their fees equal 13% of the value of the trust principal over a short period of time. Mr. Schultz stated that payments were made over two years but were for services provided over 4 years including a trial brought by Ms. Tom. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT
Off Calendar Jun-24-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz for (trustee) Scott Hodges. Steven Heller for and with (objector/beneficiary) Diana Tom.

The Court stated that the objection to the petition was reviewed and asked if there was anything additional. Mr. Heller stated that he did not file an opposition to the reply, that the trustee gave an excuse of uncertainty in regards to the lack of distribution of rental income, that the uncertainty has been removed, that the trustee has over $200,000.00 in accounts after the repairs have been paid, that the trustee is asking to retain funds to pay attorney and trustee fees now and in the future, that this is not what was agreed to in the 2013 settlement agreement and that if the court grants this petition over 10% of the trust will be distributed to attorneys. Mr. Heller further stated that Ms. Tom is retired, has no income, has exhausted her savings, has received nothing from the trust in 4 years, that the trustee has failed to maximize rents and that per his expert the Hair Salon is worth $2,600.00 per month ($2,100.00 in 2011) and is only generating $1,200.00 per month. Mr. Schultz stated that this is a simple trust, that there is 1 building with 4 tenants, all of the tenants are paying rent, that the rent is used to maintain the building, that there is no net trust income, that they agree to a distribution of principal when the renovations are complete, that they expect the work to be done in 2 weeks with new tenants in July. Mr. Schultz stated that in regards to attorney fees, Ms. Tom brought an action, that legal fees were generated in defending it, that Ms. Tom lost the trial and that they have no desire to see her in a hardship situation. Mr. Heller stated that the trial was a 1 hour hearing with Ms. Tom as the single witness and that Ms. Tom is in urgent need of funds. The court stated that it will not resolve issues that are not before the court, and took the accounting petition under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Oct-26-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Scott Hodges (Trustee). Daniel Conrad litigation Counsel for Scott Hodges. Stephen Heller for and with Diana Tom.

Mr. Heller stated that the Parties have roughed out a settlement and that they may not be able to finalize it until early/mid November. Mr. Conrad stated that they do not have a settlement yet but that they are talking, that they have a stipulation to continue the trial date, that the settlement has to do with 50% ownership and that there is no agreement regarding the accounting. Mr. Siracusa stated that the issue of rents has been resolved, that an order was issued and asked the Court to make a finding that no rents are due. Mr. Siracusa submitted a revised order which is to be reviewed by Mr. Heller by October 23, 2012. The accounting petition was taken under submission. The trial dates on the Petition to Determine Ownership of Trust Property have been moved from November 2, 2012 and November 9, 2012 to January 11, 2013 and January 18, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 pursuant to stipulation.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Parties To Consider The Terms Of The Proposed Interim Order
Off Calendar Mar-27-2012 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Natacha Kolb for Remainder Beneficiaries. Andrew Schultz for and with Scott Hodges. Daniel Conrad, Litigation Counsel for Scott Hodges. Stephen Heller for and with Diana Tom.

Mr. Hellman required this appearance to address the terms of the proposed order. He stated that Ms. Tom will withdraw her objection to pledge her share of the property for a loan if she will received a $3,000.00 monthly distribution. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions. The Court is unable to evaluate Ms. Tom's financial needs without her financial statment. With Ms. Tom's failure to cooperate for a loan, her demand for income despite the need for repair, the property needs to be sold. Mr. Conrad will go forward with discovery on the Partition Petition. Mr. Heller will file a response to the Petition for Instructions. Both petitions have been continued to April 24, 2012 at 10:30 a.m. in Dept. 204. Today's matter is off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: None.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-20-2012 Continued To Apr-24-12 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Natacha Kolb for Remainder Beneficiaries/Former Trustees. Andrew Schultz for and with Scott Hodges. Daniel Conrad Litigation Counsel for Scott Hodges. Stephen Heller for and with Diana Tom.

Mr. Heller raised the issue of the Trustee not providing Ms. Tom with financial information from October to present and not making distributions to Ms. Tom. The Court expressed concern about Ms. Tom not cooperating with the Trustee with regard to obtaining a loan and not contributing to her share of the costs of repairs, etc. Mr. Conrad advised the Court that the partition action CGC-12-517572 has been filed and served. The Court stated that there needs to be estimates of the cost of the P.G. & E. project/repairs, and for renovation of the vacant units and that the Parties need to focus on making said repairs and generating a stream of income for the Trust as well as the Beneficiaries. Mr. Schultz presented oral argument to support their positions. Ms. Tom stated that she would cooperate with a loan to complete the PG&E repairs and the renovations in the vacant units if Mr. Hodges would resign. The Court stated it was not inclined to replace Mr. Hodges. The Court proposed a detailed interim order which would allow the repairs to be made and address other issues. The terms of the proposed order were discussed. The Court will email the parties the terms of the proposed interim order. The matter was continued to March 27, 2012 at 10:30 for the parties to consider the terms of the proposed order. The Petition to Determine Ownership and Petition for Instructions were continued to 4/24/12 at 10:30.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Off Calendar Aug-23-2011 Continued To Sep-20-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz for and with Scott Hodges (Trustee). Natacha Kolb for and with Jacqueline Tom and Cathy Yih (Remainder Beneficiaries). Steve Heller and Lisa Quan for and with Diana Tom.

Mr. Schultz reported as to status of the property, status of the loan and as to communication between the Trustee and Ms. Tom. The matter was continued to September 20, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the Parties to come in and report as to how things are going.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Petition To Determine Ownership Of Trust Property, For Order Authorizing And Directing Co-Trustees To Transfer Trust Property To Petitioner
Off Calendar Aug-16-2011 Continued To Aug-23-11 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Ownership Of Trust Property, For Order Authorizing And Directing Co-Trustees To Transfer Trust Property To Petitioner
Off Calendar May-03-2011 Continued To Aug-16-11 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Natacha Kolb for and with Jacqueline Tom and for Cathy Yih (Remainder Beneficiaries). Andrew Schultz for and with Scott Hodges (Trustee). Virginia Peiser and Carolyn Lee for and with Diana Tom.

The Parties reported that have made remarkable progress, that Scott Hodges was selected, that Mr. Hodges accepted and that the prior Trustees resigned. In addition, they stated that there was the issue of turning over funds, transfer of title of the building, turning over records, the accounting, that there was a serious electrical issue that caused PG&E to turn off the power, that the Tenants were without power for a period of days, that Mr. Hodges is dealing with the issue and that PG&E needs to do a re-inspection to turn the power back on. Furthermore, they stated that Ms. Tom completed her move to the upstairs unit, that the scope of Mr. Hodges responsibilities has been drafted, that it was reviewed by Ms. Kolb and that there issues they need to address, spoke of a hearing and discovery on the issues of surcharge, Attorney's fees, Trustee's fees and the elder abuse matter and that the focus should be on renting out the other units. Mr. Schultz aslo stated that there was a serious issue with the heat sources, that the units will generate significant income when repaired, that Mr. Hodges received $14,000.00 from the prior Trustees, that there were no funds or reserves, that they will look into loans, get estimates, raised the issue of what Ms. Tom is receiving in rents and the power outage claims. Ms. Kolb's Clients are preparing an accounting which will be ready by the first week in June. The Petition to Determine Ownership of Trust Property, etc. was continued August 16, to 2011 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status of the above issues.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Determine Ownership Of Trust Property, For Order Authorizing And Directing Co-Trustees To Transfer Trust Property To Petitioner
Off Calendar Mar-02-2011 Continued To May-03-11 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Virginia Peiser and Carolyn Lee for and with Diana Tom. Natacha Kolb for and with Jackie Tom and Cathy Lee.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions. After having lengthy discussions, the Parties stipulated to the following: Ms. Peiser will propose three individual Trustees (probably Professional Fiduciaries) to Ms. Kolb by March 11, 2011. Ms. Kolb will select one of the three by March 18, 2011. The Parties will leave it up to the Trustee as to whether he or she could manage the property or hire a Property Manager. Diana Tom will move from unit 1602 to unit 1604 by April 30, 2011. There is to be no interference by any Party with regard to the Trustee's decisions. The Parties are to look at the language of the Trust to see if provisions provide a mechanism for appointment of a Successor Trustee. If not, an ex parte petition for resignation/appointment of Successor Trustee will need to be filed. The parties will also work on the scope of responsibility and decision making rights of the Successor Trustee. The issue of fees/surcharge are reserved. The Parties are to prepare a stipulation and order with regard to the above. The matter was continued to May 3, 2011 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lee.
